metadata.website_type: research and innovation conference event website

tasks.name: Purchase the cheapest 3-day conference pass with workshop access under $700
tasks.description: From the conference website, find all 3-day passes that include at least 1 workshop credit, select the cheapest option that costs less than $700, and complete the on-site payment registration flow until the reservation is created.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation link labeled "Tickets & Pricing".
On the tickets page, locate the filter for "Pass duration" and select the "3-Day" option.
In the "Includes workshops" or "Add-ons" filter section, select the option that indicates the pass "Includes at least 1 workshop credit".
Locate the sort dropdown for passes and select "Price: Low to High".
From the sorted list, click on the first 3-day pass that shows a total price under $700 to open its details page.
On the pass details page, click the "Add to cart" or "Select this pass" button.
In the cart panel or cart page, click the button labeled "Proceed to registration" or similar.

On the registration form, enter "Alex Rivera" as full name, "alex.rivera@example.com" as email, "Rivera Design Lab" as organization, and select attendance for all three conference days if a date selector is shown.

At the payment options section, choose any non-external payment choice such as "Pay on-site" or "Invoice me later" (if available), and click the final button labeled "Complete registration" or "Confirm reservation".

tasks.name: Build a 4-session agenda across 2 days with at least one keynote and one AI track session
tasks.description: Create a personal agenda with exactly 4 sessions: at least 1 keynote, at least 1 session from the "AI & Machine Learning" track, all scheduled between 09:00 and 17:00, distributed across Day 1 (June 12) and Day 2 (June 13) without overlapping start times.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Program" menu and select "Full Schedule".
In the schedule, set the day filter to "Day 1 (June 12)".
Open the "Session type" filter and select the "Keynote" option.
Within the Day 1 keynote list, choose the first keynote that starts at or after 10:00, click its title to open the details page, and then click the "Add to my schedule" button.
Use the breadcrumb or navigation link to return to the full schedule while keeping the Day 1 filter active, clear the "Session type" filter, and in the "Track" filter select "AI & Machine Learning".
From the AI & Machine Learning sessions on Day 1, choose one that starts at least 2 hours after the keynote you added, open its details page, and click "Add to my schedule".
Back on the schedule page, change the day filter to "Day 2 (June 13)", and set the time filter or slider (if available) so that it only shows sessions between 09:00 and 17:00.
On Day 2, choose any two sessions of any type whose start times are at least 1 hour apart, open each session’s details page one by one, and click "Add to my schedule" for both.
In the site header, click the "My Schedule" or "My Agenda" link to navigate to your personal agenda view showing the 4 selected sessions.

tasks.name: Add three evening networking events with free refreshments on Day 1 to your schedule
tasks.description: Identify networking events occurring on Day 1 (June 12) that start at or after 17:00 and include free refreshments, then add exactly three of these events to your personal schedule.
steps: From the homepage, click the main navigation item labeled "Social & Networking" or similar.
On the networking page, select the tab or section labeled "Events" or "Social Events".
Use the date or day filter to select "Day 1 (June 12)".
If a time filter is available, set it so that only events starting at 17:00 or later are shown (for example, set the start time to 17:00).
In the filters sidebar, open the "Perks" or "Amenities" section and select the checkbox labeled "Free refreshments" or "Drinks & snacks included".
In the "Event type" filter, select the option labeled "Networking" (and deselect other event types if necessary).

From the filtered Day 1 evening networking events list, open the first three events one by one by clicking their titles, and on each event details page click the "Add to my schedule" button before returning to the events list.

tasks.name: Choose the most advanced design thinking workshop under 3 hours and add it to your schedule
tasks.description: Among Day 2 workshops about design thinking that last 3 hours or less, select the workshop with the highest difficulty level (e.g., Advanced over Intermediate) that costs under $300, and add it to your personal schedule.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click on the "Program" menu, then select "Workshops".
On the workshops page, set the day filter to "Day 2 (June 13)".
In the workshops search field, type "design thinking" and submit the search.
Apply the duration filter by setting the maximum duration to 3 hours (or selecting the "Up to 3 hours" option).
Locate the sort dropdown and select "Price: Low to High" to order the design thinking workshops by cost.
Open the first two design thinking workshop results by clicking each workshop title so that each opens in its own tab or page.

Review the details of these design thinking workshops and decide which one has a listed difficulty level that is highest (for example, choose "Advanced" over "Intermediate") while also having a price under $300; on that chosen workshop’s page, click the "Add to my schedule" button.
Use the header link labeled "My Schedule" or "My Agenda" to navigate to your agenda where the chosen design thinking workshop has been added.

tasks.name: Bookmark two Gold-level AI tool exhibitors rated 4.0+ and book demo slots with both
tasks.description: Within the exhibitors section, filter for Gold sponsors offering AI tools, select two exhibitors with ratings of at least 4.0 stars, bookmark their booths, and schedule a 15-minute demo with each on Day 2 in the afternoon.
steps: From the homepage, click the navigation item labeled "Exhibitors & Sponsors".
On the exhibitors page, open the "Sponsor level" filter and select "Gold".
In the category or industry filter, select the option labeled "AI Tools & Platforms" or the closest AI-related category.
Use the rating filter to show only exhibitors with a rating of 4.0 stars or higher.
From the filtered list, open the first two exhibitor profiles by clicking on their names or logos.
On the first exhibitor’s profile page, click the "Bookmark" or "Save booth" icon/button to save this exhibitor for later.

On that same exhibitor page, click the "Book a demo" or "Schedule meeting" button, select "Day 2 (June 13)", choose an available 15-minute slot between 14:00 and 16:00 (for example, 14:00–14:15), and click the button to send or confirm the demo booking.

Switch to the second exhibitor’s profile page, click the "Bookmark" or "Save booth" option, then click "Book a demo" or similar, select "Day 2 (June 13)", choose a different 15-minute slot between 14:00 and 16:00 (for example, 14:30–14:45), and click the button to finalize the second demo booking.

tasks.name: Save one nearby hotel under $220/night with breakfast included and 4.0+ rating
tasks.description: Using the conference travel section, find a hotel within 1 km of the venue that costs less than $220 per night, includes breakfast, and has a rating of at least 4.0, then add that hotel to your saved options list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation link labeled "Travel & Accommodation" or similar.
On the travel page, select the tab or section labeled "Hotels".
Use the distance filter to set the maximum distance from the venue to "1 km" (or select the "Within 1 km" option).
Adjust the price slider or price filter so that the maximum nightly rate is set to $220.
In the amenities filter, check the option labeled "Breakfast included".
Use the rating filter to show only hotels with a rating of 4.0 or higher.
Set the sort order to "Distance: Nearest first".
From the resulting list, click the first hotel to open its details page and click the "Save" or "Add to my trip" button to add it to your saved hotels.

tasks.name: Add two short UX on-demand sessions from last year to your watchlist
tasks.description: Within the on-demand library, filter for sessions from the previous year in the "UX & Design" track that are 30 minutes or shorter, and add the first two such sessions to your watchlist.
steps: From the homepage, click the navigation item labeled "On-Demand Library" or "Recordings".
On the library page, set the "Year" filter to the previous conference year (for example, select "2023" if the current conference is 2024).
In the track filter, select "UX & Design".
Use the duration filter to show only sessions with a length of 30 minutes or less (for example, choose "Up to 30 minutes").
Set the sort option to "Most Popular" so that the most-watched sessions appear at the top.
From the filtered list, open the first two session recordings one by one by clicking their titles, and on each session page click the "Add to watchlist" or "Save to watchlist" button.

tasks.name: Schedule three 20-minute meetings with UX designers from different companies on Day 2
tasks.description: Use the attendee directory to find three attendees with the role "UX Designer" from three different companies and send 20-minute 1:1 meeting requests to each on Day 2 (June 13) between 13:00 and 15:00.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the "Networking" menu item, then select "Attendee Directory".
In the directory filters, set the "Role" filter to "UX Designer".
From the filtered results, open the profiles of three different attendees whose listed companies are all different by clicking on each attendee’s name.
On the first attendee’s profile, click the "Request 1:1 Meeting" or "Schedule meeting" button.
In the meeting scheduler, select "Day 2 (June 13)", set the start time to 13:00, choose a duration of 20 minutes, and click the button to send the meeting request.
Return to the second attendee’s profile tab or page, click "Request 1:1 Meeting", choose "Day 2 (June 13)", select a start time of 13:30 with a 20-minute duration, and send the request.
On the third attendee’s profile, click the meeting request button again, choose "Day 2 (June 13)", select a start time of 14:00 with a 20-minute duration, and send this final request.
Use the header navigation to open the "Meetings" or "My Networking" section where your outgoing meeting requests are listed.

entities.name: Conference
entities.description: Global conference metadata such as dates, venue, and current year.

entities.name: TicketType
entities.description: Conference ticket and pass options available for purchase.

fields.name: days_included
fields.description: Array of conference days covered by the pass; values use day_id enum from sessions.

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Shopping cart for selected tickets before registration and payment.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Array of CartItem ids or embedded CartItem objects.

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line item in the shopping cart for a specific ticket type.

entities.name: RegistrationOrder
entities.description: Completed registration record with attendee info and payment choice.

fields.name: selected_days
fields.description: Array of day_id enum values representing days the attendee will attend.

entities.name: LiveSession
entities.description: All live scheduled items: talks, keynotes, workshops, and networking events.

fields.name: perks
fields.description: Array of perk enum values such as free refreshments.

fields.name: speakers
fields.description: Array of speaker names as strings.

fields.name: hosts
fields.description: Array of host or facilitator names.

fields.name: added_to_my_schedule
fields.description: Whether this session is in the single user's personal schedule.

entities.name: OnDemandSession
entities.description: Recorded sessions available in the on-demand library.

fields.name: in_watchlist
fields.description: Whether the single user has added this recording to their watchlist.

entities.name: Exhibitor
entities.description: Exhibitors and sponsors participating in the conference.

fields.name: categories
fields.description: Array of category enum values such as ai_tools_and_platforms.

fields.name: is_bookmarked
fields.description: Whether the single user has bookmarked this exhibitor.

entities.name: Hotel
entities.description: Hotels listed in the travel & accommodation section.

fields.name: distance_km
fields.description: Distance from the conference venue in kilometers.

fields.name: amenities
fields.description: Array of hotel amenity enum values such as breakfast_included.

fields.name: is_saved
fields.description: Whether the single user has saved this hotel to their trip list.

entities.name: Attendee
entities.description: Attendees visible in the attendee directory for networking.

entities.name: Meeting
entities.description: 1:1 attendee meetings and exhibitor demo bookings.

relationships.description: Tickets are defined for a specific conference (field stored in TicketType but not explicitly listed above).
Each live session is part of a conference (field stored in LiveSession but not explicitly listed above).
On-demand recordings are associated with a conference edition/year (field stored in OnDemandSession but not explicitly listed above).
Optional link when an on-demand recording is the recording of a specific live session.
1:1 attendee meetings reference the other attendee's profile.
Exhibitor demo bookings reference an exhibitor.
Meetings take place during a specific conference (field stored in Meeting but not explicitly listed above).
Hotels are recommendations tied to a conference location (field stored in Hotel but not explicitly listed above).
Exhibitors participate in a specific conference (field stored in Exhibitor but not explicitly listed above).
